message:
feat(robot): unified agent type aliasing across all commands (R3)

- Add ResolveAgentType() function for consistent alias resolution
  - cc/claude/claude_code -> claude
  - cod/codex/codex_cli -> codex
  - gmi/gemini/gemini_cli -> gemini
- Update detectAgentType() to recognize short forms in pane titles
  - Matches "session__cc_1" as claude, etc.
  - Uses word boundary detection to avoid false positives
- Update --type filter in robot.go and ack.go to use ResolveAgentType
- Improve --type flag help text to show available aliases

Completes the Robot Command Reliability epic (ntm-0q9o):
- R1: Duration string support ✓
- R2: Standardized JSON error responses ✓
- R3: Agent type aliasing ✓ (this commit)
- R4: Graceful unimplemented feature handling ✓

Closes ntm-3vm7

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-3n9","title":"Implement agent type detection from pane content","description":"Improve agent type detection beyond pane title parsing.

## Current State
detectAgentType() in robot.go only checks pane title. This is unreliable because:
- Pane titles may not be set
- Users may use custom titles
- Agent type may not be in title

## Improved Detection
1. **Pane Title**: Check for 'claude', 'codex', 'gemini', etc.
2. **Process Name**: Check running process (claude, codex-cli, etc.)
3. **Pane Content**: Look for characteristic prompts/output:
   - Claude: '>' prompt, 'Claude' mentions
   - Codex: 'codex>' prompt, OpenAI references
   - Gemini: Gemini-specific formatting
   - Cursor: Cursor AI patterns
   - Windsurf: Windsurf patterns
   - Aider: 'aider>' prompt
4. **Environment**: Check for agent-specific env vars

## Detection Priority
Process name > Pane content > Pane title > Unknown

## Caching
Cache detected type per pane to avoid repeated content parsing. Invalidate on session reload.

## Output
```go
type AgentDetection struct {
    Type       string  // claude, codex, etc.
    Confidence float64 // 0.0-1.0
    Method     string  // how detected: process, content, title
}
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:53:43.118071-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:26:15.946452-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T03:26:15.946452-05:00","comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"ntm-3n9","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Detection Strategies

### Priority Order
1. **Process-based** (highest confidence)
   - ps aux | grep for process names: claude, codex-cli, gemini-cli
   - Check /proc/{pid}/cmdline on Linux
   - Most reliable but requires shell execution

2. **Environment-based**
   - Check for CLAUDE_*, OPENAI_*, GOOGLE_* env vars in pane
   - Can infer agent type from env configuration

3. **Content-based**
   - Scan last N lines of pane output
   - Look for characteristic patterns:
     - Claude: '>' prompt, 'Claude' in output, tool use XML
     - Codex: 'codex>' prompt, OpenAI references
     - Gemini: 'gemini>' prompt
     - Aider: 'aider>' or '/help' in prompt
   
4. **Title-based** (lowest confidence)
   - Current implementation - fallback only

### Caching Strategy
```go
type AgentTypeCache struct {
    cache map[string]CachedDetection // key: session:pane
    ttl   time.Duration              // default 60s
    mu    sync.RWMutex
}

type CachedDetection struct {
    Type       string
    Confidence float64
    Method     string
    DetectedAt time.Time
}
```

### Confidence Scores
- Process match: 0.95
- Env match: 0.85
- Content match: 0.70
- Title match: 0.50
- Unknown: 0.0","created_at":"2025-12-10T07:55:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-3ogq","title":"T6: Enhanced empty states with icons and guidance","description":"# T6: Enhanced Empty States with Icons and Guidance

## Parent Epic
[EPIC] Dashboard Visual Polish (ntm-af32)

## Problem Statement
Current empty states are minimal:
\`\`\`
No metrics yet
\`\`\`

This feels unfinished. Premium empty states:
- Include visual elements (icons)
- Explain WHY it's empty
- Guide users on what to do

## Solution
Create enhanced empty state component:

### Before
\`\`\`
No metrics yet
\`\`\`

### After
\`\`\`
         ◎
    No metrics yet
    
  Data will appear when
  agents start working
\`\`\`

## Visual Design

### Standard Empty State
\`\`\`
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                     │
│              ◎                      │
│        No metrics yet               │
│                                     │
│     Data appears when agents        │
│        start processing             │
│                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
\`\`\`

### Empty with Action
\`\`\`
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                     │
│              ○                      │
│       No command history            │
│                                     │
│    Send a prompt with 's' key       │
│     to start building history       │
│                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
\`\`\`

### Empty with External Cause
\`\`\`
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                     │
│              ◇                      │
│     Beads not initialized           │
│                                     │
│      Run 'bd init' in your          │
│     project to enable tracking      │
│                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
\`\`\`

## Implementation Details

### Component API
\`\`\`go
type EmptyStateOptions struct {
    Icon        string  // Visual icon (◎, ○, ◇, etc.)
    Title       string  // Primary message
    Description string  // Explanatory text (optional)
    Action      string  // Suggested action (optional)
    Width       int     // Available width
    Centered    bool    // Center in container (default: true)
}

func RenderEmptyState(opts EmptyStateOptions) string {
    // Render centered, styled empty state
}
\`\`\`

### Icon Meanings
| Icon | Meaning | Use Case |
|------|---------|----------|
| ◎ | Waiting | Data not yet available |
| ○ | Empty | Checked, nothing found |
| ◇ | External | Needs external action |
| ✓ | Success | Empty is good (no errors) |
| ? | Unknown | Couldn't determine |

### Panel Updates
Each panel gets appropriate empty state:

| Panel | Icon | Title | Description |
|-------|------|-------|-------------|
| Metrics | ◎ | No metrics yet | Data appears when agents start |
| History | ○ | No command history | Send prompts to build history |
| Files | ○ | No recent changes | File changes will appear here |
| Beads | ◇ | Not initialized | Run 'bd init' to enable |
| CASS | ◎ | No context found | Relevant history will appear |
| Alerts | ✓ | All clear | No alerts to display |

### Files to Create/Modify
- \`internal/tui/components/state.go\` - Enhanced empty state
- \`internal/tui/dashboard/panels/*.go\` - Use new empty states

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] EmptyStateOptions component created
- [ ] All panels use enhanced empty states
- [ ] Icons are appropriate for each context
- [ ] Descriptions are helpful (not generic)
- [ ] Centered layout looks balanced
- [ ] Visual hierarchy is clear

## Testing Notes
1. Create new session with no history
2. Verify each panel shows appropriate empty state
3. Verify empty state disappears when data arrives
4. Test at various widths for layout stability","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T15:59:39.740531-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T16:17:02.477069-05:00","comments":[{"id":21,"issue_id":"ntm-3ogq","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Build-On-Existing Infrastructure Note

**EXISTING COMPONENTS** (in `internal/tui/components/state.go`):
- `RenderState(StateOptions)` - already supports Icon, Message, Hint, Width, Align
- `EmptyState(message, width)` - convenience wrapper
- `LoadingState(message, width)` - convenience wrapper
- `ErrorState(message, hint, width)` - convenience wrapper

**WHAT THIS BEAD ADDS** (incremental value):

1. **Semantic Icon Constants**: Create named constants for icon meanings:
   ```go
   const (
       IconWaiting  = "◎"  // Data not yet available
       IconEmpty    = "○"  // Checked, nothing found
       IconExternal = "◇"  // Needs external action
       IconSuccess  = "✓"  // Empty is good (no errors)
       IconUnknown  = "?"  // Couldn't determine
   )
   ```

2. **Per-Panel Empty State Guidelines**: Document which icon/message/hint to use for each panel

3. **Consider adding Description field**: Currently Hint is used for error hints - may want separate Description for explanatory text vs action hints

**APPROACH**: Extend existing `StateOptions` rather than creating new component. The foundation is solid.","created_at":"2025-12-15T21:17:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-3qw4","title":"Add model/variant to robot output structs","description":"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T16:10:31.614499-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T16:14:37.47617-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T16:14:37.47617-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-3qw4","depends_on_id":"ntm-5zc","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-10T16:10:31.616664-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"ntm-3vm7","title":"R3: Agent type aliasing across all commands","description":"# R3: Unify Agent Type Aliasing Across Robot Flags

## Parent Epic
[EPIC] Robot Command Reliability (ntm-0q9o)

## IMPORTANT: Significant Infrastructure Already Exists

### Already Implemented:
- \`detectAgentType(title)\` in robot.go - detects from pane title
- \`DetectAgentTypeEnhanced()\` in detection.go - multi-method detection
- \`agentTypeString()\` in robot.go - converts tmux.AgentType to string
- \`AgentBadge(agentType)\` in styles/badges.go - handles cc/claude/codex/cod/gmi/gemini
- \`AgentBorderColor(agentType)\` in layout.go - handles agent colors
- \`AgentType\` enum in cli/agent_spec.go - defines AgentTypeClaude, AgentTypeCodex, AgentTypeGemini

### The Actual Gap:
Robot flag \`--type\` filter may not accept all alias forms consistently.

## Problem Statement
While most aliasing works internally, the \`--type\` flag for robot commands may not accept all forms:
\`\`\`bash
# Do all these work?
ntm --robot-send=test --type=claude --msg="hi"  # long form
ntm --robot-send=test --type=cc --msg="hi"      # short form
ntm --robot-send=test --type=claude_code --msg="hi"  # underscore form
\`\`\`

## Solution
Ensure \`--type\` flag uses centralized resolution:

### Step 1: Verify/Create Unified Resolver
Check if a single \`resolveAgentType()\` function exists. If not, create one that:
\`\`\`go
// ResolveAgentType normalizes any agent type string to canonical form.
func ResolveAgentType(input string) string {
    normalized := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(input))
    normalized = strings.ReplaceAll(normalized, "-", "_")
    
    switch normalized {
    case "cc", "claude", "claude_code":
        return "claude"
    case "cod", "codex", "codex_cli":
        return "codex"
    case "gmi", "gemini", "gemini_cli":
        return "gemini"
    // ... other types
    default:
        return normalized // Unknown type passed through
    }
}
\`\`\`

### Step 2: Use in Robot Flag Parsing
In root.go where \`--type\` is parsed:
\`\`\`go
if robotSendType != "" {
    agentTypes = strings.Split(robotSendType, ",")
    for i, t := range agentTypes {
        agentTypes[i] = ResolveAgentType(t)  // Normalize
    }
}
\`\`\`

### Files to Audit/Modify
- \`internal/robot/robot.go\` - Check type filtering logic
- \`internal/cli/root.go\` - --type flag parsing
- May need to create shared resolver if doesn't exist

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] \`--type=cc\` works same as \`--type=claude\`
- [ ] \`--type=cod\` works same as \`--type=codex\`
- [ ] \`--type=gmi\` works same as \`--type=gemini\`
- [ ] JSON output always uses canonical form
- [ ] No new enums/types created (use existing)

## Testing Notes
\`\`\`bash
# Test all alias forms work:
ntm --robot-send=test --type=cc --msg="hi"
ntm --robot-send=test --type=claude --msg="hi"
ntm --robot-send=test --type=claude_code --msg="hi"
# All should target the same panes
\`\`\`

## Integration Note for AI Agents
The canonical forms returned in JSON output are: \`claude\`, \`codex\`, \`gemini\`, \`cursor\`, \`windsurf\`, \`aider\`, \`user\`, \`unknown\`. Agents should use these forms when setting filters, though short forms will work.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T15:54:38.131535-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T16:12:17.717864-05:00"}

ntm --robot-send=test --type=claude --msg="hi"  # long form

// ResolveAgentType normalizes any agent type string to canonical form.
func ResolveAgentType(input string) string {
    normalized := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(input))
    normalized = strings.ReplaceAll(normalized, "-", "_")
    
    switch normalized {
    case "cc", "claude", "claude_code":
        return "claude"
    case "cod", "codex", "codex_cli":
        return "codex"
    case "gmi", "gemini", "gemini_cli":
        return "gemini"
    // ... other types
    default:
        return normalized // Unknown type passed through
    }
}
\`\`\`

### Step 2: Use in Robot Flag Parsing
In root.go where \`--type\` is parsed:
\`\`\`go
if robotSendType != "" {
    agentTypes = strings.Split(robotSendType, ",")
    for i, t := range agentTypes {
        agentTypes[i] = ResolveAgentType(t)  // Normalize
    }
}
\`\`\`

### Files to Audit/Modify

- May need to create shared resolver if doesn't exist

- [ ] \`--type=gmi\` works same as \`--type=gemini\`
- [ ] JSON output always uses canonical form
- [ ] No new enums/types created (use existing)

## Testing Notes
\`\`\`bash
# Test all alias forms work:
ntm --robot-send=test --type=cc --msg="hi"
ntm --robot-send=test --type=claude --msg="hi"
ntm --robot-send=test --type=claude_code --msg="hi"
# All should target the same panes
\`\`\`

## Integration Note for AI Agents
The canonical forms returned in JSON output are: \`claude\`, \`codex\`, \`gemini\`, \`cursor\`, \`windsurf\`, \`aider\`, \`user\`, \`unknown\`. Agents should use these forms when setting filters, though short forms will work.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T15:54:38.131535-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T17:52:41.844139-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T17:52:41.844139-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented unified agent type aliasing with ResolveAgentType function and detectAgentType short form support"}

@@ -588,7 +588,7 @@ func init() {
 	rootCmd.Flags().StringVar(&robotSend, "robot-send", "", "Send prompt to panes atomically (JSON output)")
 	rootCmd.Flags().StringVar(&robotSendMsg, "msg", "", "Message to send (used with --robot-send)")
 	rootCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&robotSendAll, "all", false, "Send to all panes including user (used with --robot-send)")
-	rootCmd.Flags().StringVar(&robotSendType, "type", "", "Filter by agent type: claude, codex, gemini (used with --robot-send)")

 	rootCmd.Flags().IntVar(&robotSendDelay, "delay-ms", 0, "Delay between sends in milliseconds (used with --robot-send)")

@@ -42,6 +42,7 @@ func TestDetectAgentType(t *testing.T) {
 		title    string
 		expected string
 	}{
+		// Canonical forms
 		{"claude lowercase", "claude code", "claude"},
 		{"claude uppercase", "CLAUDE", "claude"},
 		{"claude mixed", "Claude-Code", "claude"},
@@ -52,6 +53,22 @@ func TestDetectAgentType(t *testing.T) {
 		{"cursor", "cursor ide", "cursor"},
 		{"windsurf", "windsurf editor", "windsurf"},
 		{"aider", "aider assistant", "aider"},
+
+		// Short forms in pane titles (e.g., "session__cc_1")
+		{"cc short form", "myproject__cc_1", "claude"},
+		{"cc short form double underscore", "test__cc__2", "claude"},
+		{"cc short uppercase", "SESSION__CC_3", "claude"},
+		{"cod short form", "myproject__cod_1", "codex"},
+		{"cod short form double underscore", "test__cod__2", "codex"},
+		{"gmi short form", "myproject__gmi_1", "gemini"},
+		{"gmi short form double underscore", "test__gmi__2", "gemini"},
+
+		// Should NOT match short forms inside words
+		{"success not cc", "success_test", "unknown"},
+		{"accord not cc", "accord_pane", "unknown"},
+		{"decode not cod", "decode_pane", "unknown"},
+
+		// Edge cases
 		{"unknown", "bash", "unknown"},
 		{"empty", "", "unknown"},
 		{"partial match", "claud", "unknown"},
@@ -923,6 +940,60 @@ func TestAgentTypeString(t *testing.T) {
 	}
 }
 
+func TestResolveAgentType(t *testing.T) {
+	tests := []struct {
+		input    string
+		expected string
+	}{
+		// Claude aliases
+		{"claude", "claude"},
+		{"cc", "claude"},
+		{"claude_code", "claude"},
+		{"claude-code", "claude"},
+		{"CLAUDE", "claude"},
+		{"CC", "claude"},
+
+		// Codex aliases
+		{"codex", "codex"},
+		{"cod", "codex"},
+		{"codex_cli", "codex"},
+		{"codex-cli", "codex"},
+		{"CODEX", "codex"},
+		{"COD", "codex"},
+
+		// Gemini aliases
+		{"gemini", "gemini"},
+		{"gmi", "gemini"},
+		{"gemini_cli", "gemini"},
+		{"gemini-cli", "gemini"},
+		{"GEMINI", "gemini"},
+		{"GMI", "gemini"},
+
+		// Other known types
+		{"cursor", "cursor"},
+		{"windsurf", "windsurf"},
+		{"aider", "aider"},
+		{"user", "user"},
+
+		// Unknown types pass through
+		{"unknown_agent", "unknown_agent"},
+		{"custom", "custom"},
+
+		// Edge cases
+		{"  claude  ", "claude"}, // Trimming whitespace
+		{"", ""},                 // Empty string
+	}
+
+	for _, tc := range tests {
+		t.Run(tc.input, func(t *testing.T) {
+			got := ResolveAgentType(tc.input)
+			if got != tc.expected {
+				t.Errorf("ResolveAgentType(%q) = %q, want %q", tc.input, got, tc.expected)
+			}
+		})
+	}
+}
+
 // ====================
 // Test PlanOutput variations
 // ====================
